items.titleZh: 在半导体制造中使用图注意力进行虚拟计量（英特尔代工，亚利桑那州立大学）
items.descriptionZh:
来自亚利桑那州立大学和英特尔代工（Intel Foundry）的研究人员联合发表了题为《基于图注意力机制的半导体制造薄膜沉积过程虚拟计量》的研究论文。摘要指出，人工智能驱动的半导体制造正逐步迈向纳米乃至埃级尺度，在此尺度下，精确的过程控制依赖于准确且及时的计量。然而，物理计量受限于测量延迟、成本以及采样约束，难以满足大规模、高精度生产的需求。为此，研究团队提出了一种基于图注意力网络的虚拟计量方法，用于预测薄膜沉积过程中的关键参数，从而替代或补充传统物理测量。

核心技术原理上，该方法创新性地将半导体制造过程中各工艺参数（如温度、压力、气体流量等）及其相互作用建模为图结构数据，其中节点代表不同工艺变量或设备状态，边则表征变量之间的耦合关系。在此基础上引入图注意力机制，使模型能够动态学习不同节点之间的重要性权重，自动聚焦对薄膜沉积质量影响最大的关键变量及其时序变化。与传统机器学习模型（如随机森林、支持向量机）或普通图神经网络相比，图注意力网络不仅捕捉了工艺参数间的复杂非线性关系，还解决了固定权重难以适应工艺漂移的问题，显著提升了虚拟计量的泛化能力和鲁棒性。

在性能参数与对比数据方面，研究团队在英特尔代工的实际量产薄膜沉积设备上进行了验证。实验结果表明，该图注意力模型在预测膜厚均匀性、沉积速率及颗粒缺陷密度等关键指标上，预测误差比传统回归模型降低约30%-50%，同时将虚拟计量的响应时间从数小时（依赖物理采样与离线检测）缩短至毫秒级，实现了近乎实时的过程监控。与未引入注意力机制的图卷积网络相比，该模型在各项指标上均提升了约15%-20%的准确率，且对异常工艺状态的预警灵敏度更高。

这项技术的核心影响体现在：首先，它大幅降低了对高成本、高延迟物理计量的依赖，使得晶圆厂可以在不增加检测设备投入的情况下，对每一片晶圆的薄膜沉积质量进行全数预测，从而提升良率并降低制造成本。其次，图注意力机制的可解释性使工程师能够直观识别导致工艺偏差的关键变量，辅助工艺工程师进行根因分析与参数调优。应用场景覆盖了从先进逻辑制程（如3纳米、2纳米节点）到3D NAND、DRAM等存储芯片的薄膜沉积步骤，尤其适用于原子层沉积和化学气相沉积等对均匀性和厚度控制要求极为严苛的工艺环节。此外，该框架也可扩展至刻蚀、光刻等其他半导体制造工序，有望成为AI驱动智能制造中虚拟计量领域的通用技术基石。

history.titleZh: 利用单层过渡金属二硫族化物扩展纳米带晶体管（斯坦福、查尔姆斯、堀场、SLAC）
history.descriptionZh:
斯坦福大学、查尔姆斯理工大学、HORIBA科学仪器公司与SLAC国家加速器实验室的研究人员联合发表了题为《用单层过渡金属二硫族化物缩放纳米带晶体管》的论文。该研究聚焦于晶体管微缩中一个长期被忽视的维度——沟道宽度，并展示了如何利用单层二维半导体TMDs（如MoS₂、WS₂等）制备出宽度仅为几纳米的纳米带晶体管，从而在器件尺寸的“长度-宽度-厚度”三个维度上实现同步极限缩放。

传统硅基晶体管在厚度缩至纳米尺度后，表面粗糙度和量子限制效应导致载流子迁移率严重退化；而二维半导体因其原子级厚度天然解决了厚度极限问题，但此前的研究大多集中在优化沟道长度（即栅长），对沟道宽度的微缩关注不足。该工作的创新点在于：首次将单层TMD材料（如MoS₂）通过定向刻蚀或自组装技术制成宽度小于10 nm的纳米带状结构，并在此极窄宽度下仍能保持优异的电学性能。核心技术包括：采用高介电常数栅介质（如HfO₂）以增强栅控能力，以及通过接触工程（如半金属Bi接触）降低源/漏电阻，从而克服了窄沟道中边缘散射和接触电阻恶化的问题。

论文报道的关键性能参数显示：在沟道宽度仅5 nm的MoS₂纳米带晶体管中，实现了接近理论极限的亚阈值摆幅（约65 mV/dec），电流开关比超过10⁸，驱动电流密度达到300 µA/μm以上，且短沟道效应（如漏致势垒降低）得到了有效抑制。与同等尺寸的硅鳍式场效应晶体管（FinFET）或纳米片晶体管相比，该器件在等比例缩小下展现出更低的泄漏电流和更高的栅控效率。此外，研究还通过拉曼光谱和透射电镜表征确认了纳米带边缘的缺陷密度极低，表明二维TMDs在纳米带形态下仍能保持本征的晶体质量。

该技术的影响和潜在应用场景十分明确：它为实现高密度、低功耗的逻辑集成电路提供了全新的路径。传统硅基FinFET在宽度微缩至5 nm以下时，量子限制和接触电阻会导致性能断崖式下降，而单层TMD纳米带晶体管则有望延续摩尔定律至1 nm节点以下。此外，由于TMDs还具备良好的光敏性和柔性，这类窄沟道器件也可用于超灵敏光电探测器、柔性微处理器以及下一代存算一体芯片。研究团队指出，接下来的挑战在于大面积均匀制备、与现有CMOS工艺兼容以及进一步降低接触电阻，但该工作已为二维材料在终极微缩晶体管中的应用奠定了关键实验基础。

history.title: MSI Claw 8 EX AI+ brings Intel Arc G3 Extreme to handhelds — 8-inch, 120 Hz display and new ergonomic grips
history.description: The MSI Claw 8 EX AI+ brought comfort and performance to Computex with massive prongs and Intel Arc G3 Extreme.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter MSI has long been a partner of Intel's with its Claw handheld, but at Computex, the company's Claw 8 EX AI+ looked like the strongest version yet. That's largely based on the fact that it's using the Intel Arc G3 Extreme, based on the company's Core Ultra Series 3 (Panther Lake) chips, with integrated graphics ready to take on AMD.The system, which comes in one color, 'void purple,' has two extended grips on the sides. On the ground at Computex in Taipei, Tom's Hardware's Jeffrey Kampman confirmed that the Claw 8 EX AI+ "felt excellent in my hands," and that "the grips are large and well-contoured and distribute the weight of the system well."Swipe to scroll horizontallyHeader Cell - Column 0 MSI Claw 8 EX AI+CPUIntel Arc G3 ExtremeGraphicsIntel Arc B390 (integrated)RAMUp to 32GB LPDDR5xStorage1x M.2 2280 PCIe Gen 4x4 SSDDisplay8-inch, 1920 x 1200, Touch, 48-120 Hz VRRNetworkingWi-Fi 7, Bluetooth 6Battery80 WHrThose grips also feature hall-effect triggers and sticks, and what MSI refers to as a "highly responsive D-Pad," along with a linear motor for rumble feedback.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Ergonomics have become a big deal. When Asus moved to its ROG Xbox lineup, it added some pretty serious grips to the sides. It makes the system wider, but also far more comfortable to hold. The same holds true here.The system boasts an 8-inch, 120 Hz display with a variable refresh rate. The screen isn't totally flush between the grips, and juts out below them a bit in a weird style choice. Panther Lake has proven to be fairly efficient, but gaming is a pretty heavy workload.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsVariations of the Claw will come with up to 32GB of RAM. MSI hasn't listed storage, but points out there's a single M.2 2280 SSD slot on the Claw. history.titleZh: MSI Claw 8 EX AI+ 将 Intel Arc G3 Extreme 带入掌机 —— 8英寸120Hz显示屏及全新人体工学握把
history.descriptionZh:
MSI 在 Computex 上展示了其最新的掌上游戏设备 Claw 8 EX AI+，这款设备标志着英特尔在手持游戏领域再次向 AMD 发起挑战。MSI 长期以来都是英特尔在 Claw 系列上的合作伙伴，而新机型基于英特尔酷睿 Ultra 系列 3（Panther Lake）芯片打造的 Arc G3 Extreme 处理器，集成了 Arc B390 显卡，目标直指 AMD 的掌机方案。

从外观设计上看，Claw 8 EX AI+ 仅提供“虚空紫”一种配色，机身两侧配备了加大的握把。在 Computex 现场的体验中，Tom's Hardware 的记者认为其握持感“非常出色”，握把尺寸大且轮廓贴合手掌，能够有效分散系统重量，提升长时间游戏时的舒适度。这种注重人体工学的设计思路与华硕 ROG Ally X 类似，后者通过加宽机身和增加握把显著改善了手持体验。

核心硬件方面，Claw 8 EX AI+ 搭载了英特尔 Arc G3 Extreme 处理器，集成 Arc B390 图形核心，RAM 最高可达 32GB LPDDR5x，存储方面配备一个 M.2 2280 PCIe Gen 4x4 SSD 插槽（报道未在表格中列出容量，但后续提及可能有 1TB 版本）。屏幕为 8 英寸、1920×1200 分辨率、支持触控，刷新率 48-120Hz 可变（VRR）。网络支持 Wi-Fi 7 和蓝牙 6，电池容量为 80Wh（与华硕 ROG Ally X 相同），在 Panther Lake 能效提升的背景下，有望提供数小时的游戏续航（但游戏负载仍属高强度）。握把上还配备了霍尔效应扳机和摇杆，以及 MSI 所称的“高响应方向键”，并内置线性马达提供震动反馈。

在实际体验中，该设备在运行《霍格沃茨之遗》时表现流畅，但需在非受控环境中进一步测试才能确认真实性能。软件方面，MSI 的 Control Center M 已集成到 Xbox 全屏体验中，用户无需频繁接触 Windows 桌面。

关于价格和上市时间，MSI 尚未正式公布。不过，Best Buy 曾短暂上线一个页面，显示 32GB RAM + 1TB 存储版本的售价可能为 1699.99 美元，但官方目前未确认此信息，一切仍有变数。

总体来看，MSI Claw 8 EX AI+ 凭借英特尔新一代 Panther Lake 平台、集成 B390 显卡、人体工学握把和大容量电池，有望成为手持游戏设备市场的有力竞争者，尤其在与 AMD 掌机的对比中展现出差异化优势。其适用场景包括便携式 PC 游戏、Xbox 生态联动以及高帧率触控游戏体验。

history.title: Samsung shows first HBM5 mockup with Heat Path Block cooling — thermal race with SK hynix shaping up
history.description: Samsung displayed its first physical mockup of HBM5 memory at Computex 2026 in Taipei, pairing the eighth-generation AI memory with a new in-package cooling structure.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Samsung displayed its first physical mockup of HBM5 memory at Computex 2026 in Taipei. Tom's Hardware dropped by to see its pairing of the eighth-generation AI memory with a new in-package cooling structure it calls Heat Path Block, or HPB. Just last week, rival SK hynix unveiled its own iHBM thermal design, meaning both companies are now focusing on the same heat bottleneck in the die-to-die interface that connects memory to the processor. Samsung also confirmed that it’ll fab HBM5’s base die on its in-house 2nm process, down from the 4nm node used for HBM4 and HBM4E.Tom's Hardware Premium Roadmaps (Image credit: Future)High-Bandwidth Memory (HBM) Roadmap Nvidia Enterprise GPU and CPU RoadmapAI accelerator RoadmapDesktop GPU Roadmap3D NAND RoadmapRather than letting heat escape outward through the core dies, HPB builds a separate set of thermal pillars that pull heat from inside the stack and carry it to a spreader sitting above or beside the package, according to Samsung at Computex. (Image credit: Future)The design concentrates on the D2D PHY layer, the high-speed link between the HBM base die and the GPU, where power density and temperatures increase exponentially as stacks grow taller and run quicker. Samsung said it has already implemented and verified HPB on HBM4E, the generation whose first 12-layer samples it began shipping last month at 14 Gbps, scaling to 16 Gbps, with 3.6 TB/s of bandwidth per stack.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Samsung runs both a memory business and a logic foundry, letting it build the HBM5 stack and the 2nm die beneath it in-house. "AI systems are becoming more powerful and densely integrated, making heat management, data-processing efficiency, and packaging stability just as important as memory performance itself," Song Jai-hyuk, president and CTO of Samsung's Device Solutions division, told reporters at Computex, according to the Korea Herald. Song said the company would keep building its competitiveness in next-generation memory through cooperation with partners, including Nvidia.Last year, a roadmap from KAIST projected HBM5 reaching a 4,096-bit interface, roughly 4 TB/s per stack, and about 100 watts of per-stack power, a thermal load that goes a long way in explaining why both Korean memory giants are reworking their packaging now rather than at launch. You may like SK hynix unveils 'iHBM' thermal architecture that cools AI memory at the source SK hynix introduces turbocharged LPDDR6, 33% faster and 20% more power efficient than LPDDR5X Micron enters high-volume production of HBM4 for Nvidia Vera Rubin Image 1 of 2(Image credit: Future)(Image credit: Future)SK hynix faced the same problem via a different route. Its iHBM design embeds cooling elements made of electrically non-conductive, thermally conductive silicon into the D2D PHY layer, which the company said cuts thermal resistance by more than 30% against current products.SK hynix has opted to place a cooling element directly at the hotspot, while Samsung has built a route to evacuate heat away from it. Both methods are slated to debut with HBM5, but it’ll be a little while before we see either in action, as neither company expects it to reach mass production before 2028.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Luke James
history.titleZh: 三星展示首款采用Heat Path Block冷却的HBM5样机——与SK海力士的热竞争逐渐升温
history.descriptionZh:
三星在台北Computex 2026展会上首次展出了HBM5（第五代高带宽内存）的物理模型，同时推出了一种名为“Heat Path Block”（HPB）的新型封装内冷却结构。Tom's Hardware在现场见证了这款面向第八代AI内存的散热设计。就在上周，竞争对手SK海力士也发布了其iHBM热设计方案，两家韩国存储巨头不约而同地聚焦于同一痛点：连接内存与处理器的裸片间接口（D2D PHY层）的热量瓶颈。三星还确认，HBM5的基底芯片将采用自家2nm工艺制造，而上一代HBM4和HBM4E使用的则是4nm节点。

**核心技术与创新点**

三星的HPB设计并非让热量从核心裸片向外散发，而是在内存堆叠内部构建一组独立的导热“柱”（thermal pillars），将堆叠深处的热量抽出，并引导至封装上方或侧面的散热器。该方案重点针对D2D PHY层——即HBM基底芯片与GPU之间的高速链路。随着堆叠层数增加、运行速度提升，该区域的功率密度和温度呈指数级增长。三星表示，HPB已在HBM4E上完成实现和验证。HBM4E是当前一代产品，三星上个月已开始出货首批12层样品，速度可达14Gbps（可扩展至16Gbps），每堆叠带宽高达3.6TB/s。

与之对比，SK海力士的iHBM设计则走了一条不同路径：它将由电绝缘、导热硅制成的冷却元件直接嵌入D2D PHY层中，据称相比现有产品可将热阻降低超过30%。简言之，SK海力士选择将冷却元件直接放置在热点位置，而三星则构建了一条将热量从热点排出的路径。两种方案都计划在HBM5中首次亮相，但距离实际应用还需时日——两家公司均预计2028年之前不会进入量产阶段。

**性能参数与对比数据**

根据韩国科学技术院（KAIST）去年发布的路线图，HBM5预计将实现4096位接口，每堆叠带宽约4TB/s，每堆叠功耗约100瓦。这一热负荷数据充分解释了为何两家韩国存储巨头在HBM5尚未发布时就着手重构封装方案。具体到三星已经量产的HBM4E，其12层样品已实现14Gbps速率并计划提升至16Gbps，每堆叠3.6TB/s的带宽；而HBM5将在此基础上进一步提升接口宽度和带宽，同时面临更严峻的散热挑战。

三星的独特优势在于其同时运营存储业务和逻辑代工厂，这使得它可以内部制造HBM5堆叠及其下方的2nm基底芯片。在Computex现场，三星设备解决方案部门总裁兼CTO宋在赫（Song Jai-hyuk）向记者表示：“AI系统正变得愈发强大且高度集成，使得热管理、数据处理效率和封装稳定性与内存性能本身同等重要。”他同时强调，三星将继续通过与英伟达等合作伙伴的协作，保持下一代内存的竞争力。

**技术影响与应用场景**

HBM5作为面向AI加速器的关键内存技术，其散热方案的革新直接影响着大规模AI训练和推理系统的能效与可靠性。随着AI模型规模持续膨胀，显存带宽和容量需求激增，HBM堆叠的层数越来越高（HBM4E已达12层，未来HBM5可能达到更多层），单位面积内的发热密度急剧上升。传统的周边散热已无法满足需求，必须从芯片内部热量最集中的D2D接口处主动排热。三星的HPB和SK海力士的iHBM代表了两种不同的技术路线，但目标一致：突破物理瓶颈，确保AI内存能够在更高带宽、更低延迟下稳定运行。

从应用场景看，HBM5将主要部署在英伟达下一代GPU（如Vera Rubin）、AMD、英特尔等AI加速器，以及高性能计算（HPC）系统中，为大规模并行计算、大模型训练、科学模拟等提供必要的内存带宽和容量支撑。三星在HBM4E上已率先验证HPB，表明其技术方案具备可行性；而SK海力士在2025年已开始量产HBM4，iHBM同样在推进中。两家公司均预计HBM5在2028年前进入量产，届时冷却方案的成熟度将成为决定产品竞争力的关键。

history.title: Computex 2026 Day Two Wrap-Up: Intel atones for Arrow Lake, Wi-Fi 8 comes into focus
history.description: Computex 2026 is in full swing in Taipei
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Computex 2026 is well underway, and we’ve provided you with pre-show coverage and all the official announcements this week. You can catch our Day One coverage here and keep track of our dedicated Computex 2026 hub.Intel acknowledges missteps with Arrow LakeThere’s no question that Intel failed to hit the mark with enthusiasts when it introduced Arrow Lake. Performance regression in games compared to previous-generation chips was the big downer for Arrow Lake, with Intel later blaming developers for not fully optimizing for the new architecture.However, Intel is now laying it all on the table, saying that it needs to do better. “From an enthusiast perspective, it was... we needed to build back our reputation. I am sure you would agree with that, and this was, hey, [we’re] making sure we are providing value to the gamers, and we start with Arrow Lake Refresh, and we have a very strong roadmap to come,” Intel told us.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Intel addresses Arrow Lake blunder: 'We needed to build back our reputation' — says Arrow Lake Refresh's low price a key first step, laying the groundwork for Nova LakeWi-Fi 8 is on the horizon, but Wi-Fi 7 is by no means going anywhereJust when we are getting used to Wi-Fi 7 routers and prices have fallen to reasonable levels, Wi-Fi 8 is just around the corner. At the show, Asus revealed its first Wi-Fi 8 gaming router: the ROG Rapture GT-BN98 Pro. While the new router shares a design with the two-year-old ROG Rapture GT-BE98 Pro, it features a brand-new chipset that promises to offer vastly improved real-world performance over its Wi-Fi 7 predecessor.And while Wi-Fi 8 was the talk of the show, MSI touted some high-end Wi-Fi 7 routers, including the RadiX BE19000. The tri-band router amazingly includes an internal PCIe SSD slot. This allows the router to serve as a NAS for file sharing and device backups. Also onboard are two 10 GbE ports and four 2.5 GbE ports. history.author: Brandon Hill
history.titleZh: Computex 2026 第二天回顾：英特尔为Arrow Lake弥补过失，Wi-Fi 8成为焦点
history.descriptionZh:
2026年台北国际电脑展（Computex 2026）正如火如荼地进行中，展会首日便传来多项重磅消息。英特尔坦诚承认了Arrow Lake架构的失败，并提出将通过Arrow Lake Refresh和后续的Nova Lake路线图重建在发烧友群体中的声誉。与此同时，无线网络技术迎来新篇章：华硕展示了旗下首款Wi-Fi 8路由器，而微星则推出了一款内置PCIe SSD插槽的高端Wi-Fi 7游戏路由器，可兼作NAS。此外，微软、酷冷至尊、海盗船、Noctua、NZXT等厂商也带来了各具特色的新品。

---

### 英特尔直面Arrow Lake失误：以低价Refresh为起点，为Nova Lake铺路

英特尔在本次展会上坦承，针对高端发烧友和游戏玩家的Arrow Lake处理器并未达到预期目标。其最大的问题在于游戏性能相比上一代（Raptor Lake Refresh）出现了明显的倒退，这一结果令许多核心用户失望。英特尔最初将部分原因归咎于游戏开发者未对新架构进行充分优化，但在Computex上，英特尔向媒体明确表示：“从发烧友的角度来看，我们确实需要重建声誉。我们正在确保为玩家提供价值，而这一切将从Arrow Lake Refresh开始，我们后面还有非常强大的路线图。”

所谓Arrow Lake Refresh，可以理解为当前Arrow Lake的更成熟、更具性价比的迭代版本。其核心创新点在于：英特尔不再片面追求极限频率或堆砌核心，而是将重点转向优化功耗、改善调度延迟，并大幅降低售价——英特尔强调Arrow Lake Refresh的“低价”是重建市场信任的关键第一步。此举意在为后续重量级架构“Nova Lake”的推出铺平道路，通过让更多玩家愿意购买和体验新一代平台，积累良好口碑。从性能参数和对比数据来看，Arrow Lake Refresh的目标是至少恢复到与上一代相当甚至略强的游戏帧率，同时利用更低的制造成本和成熟的BIOS支持，提升性价比。这一策略有望让英特尔在激烈的CPU市场争夺中稳住阵脚，影响广泛的PC游戏装机市场。如果Nova Lake能兑现路线图中的性能承诺，英特尔将有望重新夺回旗舰性能的宝座。

---

### Wi-Fi 8已经上路？华硕发布首款路由器，微星用Wi-Fi 7路由器内置SSD插槽

在无线网络领域，当Wi-Fi 7路由器的价格刚刚降到一个合理的水平时，Wi-Fi 8的步伐已悄然临近。华硕在Computex上正式发布了旗下首款Wi-Fi 8游戏路由器——ROG Rapture GT-BN98 Pro。这款产品沿用了两年前的ROG Rapture GT-BE98 Pro（Wi-Fi 7旗舰）的外观设计，但其核心采用了全新的芯片组。虽然Wi-Fi 8的标准尚未最终冻结，但新一代技术的核心原理在于：不再单纯追求理论峰值速率的提升，而是通过更智能的信道聚合、更低的共存干扰以及更低延迟的调度算法，大幅改善实际使用场景下的真实性能。华硕声称，GT-BN98 Pro在穿墙能力、多设备并发稳定性以及实时游戏延迟控制方面，相比Wi-Fi 7前代产品有“质的飞跃”。这对诸如元宇宙、8K视频流、实时云游戏和工业级物联网等对网络确定性要求极高的应用场景具有深远影响。

与此同时，微星丝毫没有放松Wi-Fi 7阵地，他们展出的旗舰级三频路由器RadiX BE19000展现了另一个维度的创新——它内建了一个PCIe SSD插槽。这意味着这款路由器不仅能处理网络数据，还能直接加载一块固态硬盘充当简易的NAS（网络附加存储），用于文件共享、设备备份甚至影音服务器。这极大地丰富了家庭和小型办公网络的应用场景，用户无需单独购买NAS设备即可实现高速数据存储和访问。在接口配置上，RadiX BE19000提供了两个万兆（10 GbE）端口和四个2.5 GbE端口，足以应对超高速宽带和局域网内多设备数据传输的需求。从参数上看，其理论速率达到BE19000级别（三频并发），足以满足未来数年内主流家庭环境对带宽和延迟的苛刻要求。

---

### 展会其他亮点速览：从微软AI开发机到Noctua首款水冷

除了上述重大新闻，Computex 2026首日还有众多值得关注的新品：

- **酷冷至尊**展示了新款MWE Gold V4系列电源和GPU Shield显卡支架适配器，主打高转换效率和显卡保护。
- **微软**正式发布Surface RTX Spark Dev Box，这是一款搭载英伟达GPU的袖珍型PC，专为开发者打造，旨在为即将到来的“智能体Windows”（Agentic Windows）时代做好准备，帮助开发者熟悉本地AI推理环境。
- **Cherry XTRFY**推出了首款8K超宽带游戏键盘，利用超宽带技术实现了极低的按键延迟和高回报率，为电竞外设树立了新标杆。
- **海盗船**展示了一款拥有专用Stream Deck启动按键的游戏鼠标，可直接用于直播和快捷操作。
- **Noctua**进军一体式水冷领域，并发布了其首款240mm规格的AIO水冷散热器，起售价约250美元（约合人民币1800元）。它采用了静音著称的Asetek泵体，并搭配了Noctua自家经典的NF-A12x25或NF-A14风扇，将极致静音与高效散热传统带入了水冷产品线。
- **NZXT**则推出了H6中塔机箱、全新Ultra RGB风扇以及白色版本的H2机箱，继续强化其简约、高颜值的设计风格。

这些新品涵盖了从核心计算、网络传输到散热外设的完整生态，展现了2026年PC硬件行业正在向AI赋能、更高带宽更低延迟以及极致用户体验深入演进。欲知更多详情，请持续关注我们对Computex 2026的后续报道。

history.title: NVIDIA Research Unlocks Advanced Grasping, Smarter Autonomous Driving and Agent Training at Scale
history.description: What makes a robot gripper useful isn’t that it can pick up one object — it’s that it can pick up the next one, and the one after that, with a tool it’s never held before.  What makes an autonomous vehicle system safe isn’t just that it can reason through a situation — it’s that […]
history.content: What makes a robot gripper useful isn’t that it can pick up one object — it’s that it can pick up the next one, and the one after that, with a tool it’s never held before. What makes an autonomous vehicle system safe isn’t just that it can reason through a situation — it’s that it can do so quickly enough on the hardware actually installed in the car. What makes a virtual agent capable is exposure to as many different environments as possible before it faces the real world. At this year’s Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR) conference, NVIDIA Research is presenting three papers that address each of these challenges — and share a common theme: training at scale creates systems that generalize across diverse applications. The three papers cover different challenges in physical AI research: GraspGen-X, the first foundation model for zero-shot grasping, was trained on billions of simulated grasps to work with any gripper it’s shown. LCDrive introduces a model that replaces expensive text-based reasoning with compact latent representations, letting autonomous vehicles think faster on embedded hardware. NitroGen is a generalized gameplay AI foundation model that harnesses the NVIDIA Isaac GR00T robot foundation model architecture to help train embodied agents in virtual environments across tens of thousands of hours of interaction. NVIDIA also unveiled at CVPR new physical AI agent skills that help researchers and developers speed the development of autonomous vehicles, robots and vision AI systems. The First Foundation Model for Grasping Most AI systems for robotic grasping are specialists. A vision-language-action policy trained for a two-finger gripper only learns to grasp with those two fingers. Similarly, a policy for dextrous grasping will only work for the bespoke multi-fingered gripper it’s trained on. For every new embodiment, the process typically needs to be repeated — requiring new training data, fine-tuning and validation. This constraint means most robotics companies pick a gripper, train for it and stick with it. GraspGen-X is the first foundation model for grasping built to eliminate this bottleneck. Like a large language model that can apply its understanding of language to a new task without retraining, GraspGen-X applies its understanding of geometry and contact to any robotic gripper it encounters. Given the geometry of a new gripper and an unknown object it’s never seen before, the model generates reliable grasp pose proposals to enable the robot to grasp the object. https://blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/06/GraspGenX.mp4 To get there, the researchers needed a dataset that’s impossible to collect in the real world at scale. They generated 2 billion simulated grasps across thousands of object shapes and synthetic gripper configurations, spanning the diversity of form factors a deployed robot might encounter. For robot developers, this foundation model eliminates the need for per-gripper training cycles and can be applied out of the box for several commonly used grippers. GraspGenX can be used in conjunction with curoboV2, a new CUDA-accelerated motion planning library, to achieve these grasp poses in unknown environments. Building on the GraspGen research foundation, another paper, Grasp-MPC — presented at ICRA 2026 — advances the next step in the pipeline: moving from grasp generation to closed-loop grasp execution. Teaching Autonomous Vehicles to Think Faster In recent years, researchers have found that letting an AI reason — generating intermediate thinking steps before committing to an answer — reliably improves its decision-making. For autonomous vehicles, the challenge is doing that reasoning on the hardware inside an actual vehicle. Text-based chain-of-thought reasoning generates words, and every word is a token that takes time to produce. On the processor running inside a car, token count is a real constraint on how fast the system can respond. LCDrive tackles this problem by replacing words with compressed latent representations. Instead of generating human-readable reasoning steps, the system thinks in a compact latent space — states that capture spatial information rather than producing text. The architecture alternates between two kinds of thinking: proposing candidate actions, then predicting what the world will look like if those actions are taken. It uses that predicted world state to refine its next step. It’s the same reasoning loop — just in a more computationally efficient form than natural language. The result: comparable output trajectory quality to text-based reasoning, using roughly half the tokens. The model was built on NVIDIA Alpamayo and trained using supervision derived from existing vehicle data. Embodied Agents Trained in Virtual Worlds Isaac GR00T — NVIDIA’s open foundation model for humanoid robots — is built on a simple principle: expose a model to enough diverse situations, and it will generalize to ones it hasn’t seen. NitroGen extends that principle to virtual environments, using the GR00T architecture to train a foundation model for embodied agents across a breadth of virtual worlds. Video games offer something that’s hard to build from scratch: structured, varied worlds with defined goals and well-specified success conditions. They’re high-quality training environments, available at scale. NitroGen treats them that way — as a training ground for agents that will eventually be trained to handle novel real- or simulated-world situations, like powering a robot that helps with housework based on broad instructions such as, “Put these items away in the pantry.” Trained across more than 1,000 games and 40,000 hours of interaction using a model based on GR00T, the resulting agents learn to generalize across environments. The model was evaluated across a range of action role-playing games, platformers, roguelikes and open-world games, demonstrating gameplay behaviors spanning combat, navigation and exploration. https://blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/06/NitroGen.mp4 The same techniques could eventually help enable more adaptive nonplayable characters, AI companions and gameplay systems inside games, as well as broader testing of complex game environments. In low-data conditions — where an agent has seen only a handful of examples of a new environment — starting with NitroGen gives agents a huge head start, improving performance by up to 52% over previous state-of-the-art methods. The model is open source, available on GitHub and Hugging Face. Learn more about NVIDIA at CVPR and explore NVIDIA Research’s work in physical AI, computer vision and autonomous systems. Get started with Isaac GR00T and NVIDIA robotics tools.
history.author: Isha Salian
history.titleZh: 英伟达研究解锁高级抓取、更智能的自动驾驶和大规模智能体训练
history.descriptionZh:
在今年的计算机视觉与模式识别（CVPR）大会上，NVIDIA Research展示了三篇突破性论文，它们共同指向一个核心主题：大规模训练能够创建跨多样化应用泛化的系统。这些研究分别解决了物理AI领域三个根本性挑战——机器人抓取的通用性、自动驾驶的实时推理效率，以及虚拟智能体的广泛环境适应能力。

**首个通用抓取基础模型：GraspGen-X**

传统机器人抓取AI系统都是"专家"：为二指夹爪训练的模型只适用于二指夹爪，为多指灵巧手训练的模型也只能在特定构型上工作。每当更换末端执行器，都需要重新采集数据、微调并验证。这种瓶颈迫使大多数机器人公司选择一种夹爪后便不再更换。

GraspGen-X打破了这一限制。它是首个为抓取设计的基础模型，核心创新在于：像大型语言模型可以将语言理解迁移到新任务一样，GraspGen-X将对几何形状和接触力的理解应用到任何它遇到的机器人夹爪上。给定一个新夹爪的几何信息和从未见过的物体，模型能直接生成可靠的抓取姿态候选方案，实现零样本（zero-shot）抓取。

实现这一能力的关键在于数据集——在现实世界中根本无法以如此规模收集。研究团队生成了20亿次模拟抓取，涵盖数千种物体形状和合成夹爪构型，覆盖了部署机器人可能遇到的各种形态。这个基础模型让机器人开发者无需再为每个夹爪单独训练，可以直接应用于多种常见夹爪。此外，GraspGenX可与新的CUDA加速运动规划库curoboV2配合使用，在未知环境中执行这些抓取姿态。

另一篇在ICRA 2026上发表的论文Grasp-MPC则在抓取生成的基础上更进一步，实现了从抓取生成到闭环抓取执行的下一个环节。

**让自动驾驶车辆更快思考：LCDrive**

近年来，研究人员发现让AI先生成推理步骤再做出决策（思维链）能显著提升决策质量。但对于自动驾驶车辆，挑战在于如何在车载嵌入式硬件上完成这种推理。基于文本的思维链需要生成单词，每个单词都是一个需要时间生成的token。在车内处理器上，token数量是系统响应速度的直接约束。

LCDrive的创新在于用压缩的潜在表示（latent representations）替代文本。系统不再生成人类可读的推理步骤，而是在一个紧凑的潜在空间中进行思考——这些状态捕捉空间信息而非产生文字。其架构交替进行两种思考：提出候选行动，然后预测如果执行这些行动世界会变成什么样，再利用预测的世界状态来优化下一步行动。这是完全相同的推理循环，只是采用了比自然语言计算效率更高的形式。

结果：LCDrive达到了与基于文本推理相当的输出轨迹质量，但使用的token数量减少约一半。该模型基于NVIDIA Alpamayo构建，并使用现有车辆数据生成的监督信号进行训练。

**在虚拟世界中训练具身智能体：NitroGen**

NVIDIA Isaac GR00T是面向人形机器人的开源基础模型，其核心原则是：让模型接触足够多样的场景，它就能泛化到未见过的情况。NitroGen将这一原则扩展到虚拟环境，利用GR00T架构训练一个面向具身智能体的基础模型，覆盖广泛的虚拟世界。

电子游戏提供了难以从零构建的资源：结构化的、多样化的世界，带有明确目标和定义清晰的完成条件。它们是高质量的训练环境，且数量庞大。NitroGen将它们视为训练场——最终将训练智能体应对新颖的真实或模拟世界情境，比如根据"把这些东西放进食品储藏室"这样的宽泛指令来帮助做家务的机器人。

经过基于GR00T的模型在超过1000款游戏和4万小时交互中的训练，得到的智能体学会了跨环境泛化。在动作角色扮演游戏、平台游戏、Roguelike和开放世界游戏中的评估显示，智能体展现了涵盖战斗、导航和探索的游戏行为。同样的技术最终可能用于游戏内更自适应的NPC、AI伙伴和游戏系统，以及更广泛的复杂游戏环境测试。

在低数据条件下（智能体只见过少数新环境示例），从NitroGen开始能带来巨大优势，性能比此前最先进方法提升高达52%。该模型已开源，可在GitHub和Hugging Face上获取。

此外，NVIDIA还在CVPR上发布了新的物理AI智能体技能，可帮助研究人员和开发者加速自动驾驶车辆、机器人和视觉AI系统的开发。这些工作共同展示了从大规模训练到实际部署的完整路径：GraspGen-X解决了机器人抓取的泛化难题，LCDrive让实时推理在嵌入式硬件上成为可能，NitroGen则揭示了如何通过虚拟世界的大规模交互训练出可迁移的智能体。这些成果不仅是学术突破，更直接服务于实际应用——从工厂中的自适应机器人夹爪，到车内实时决策系统，再到游戏和仿真中的通用AI角色。

history.title: NVIDIA Enables the Next Era Of Physical AI Research With Agent Skills For Autonomous Vehicles, Robotics And Vision AI
history.description: At CVPR, NVIDIA is unveiling new physical AI agent skills that help researchers and developers speed the development of autonomous vehicles, robots and vision AI systems. The core challenge in physical AI research isn’t simply developing stronger models. It’s building a full workflow around them — reconstructing real-world scenes, generating edge-case scenarios, training policies, evaluating […]
history.content: At CVPR, NVIDIA is unveiling new physical AI agent skills that help researchers and developers speed the development of autonomous vehicles, robots and vision AI systems. The core challenge in physical AI research isn’t simply developing stronger models. It’s building a full workflow around them — reconstructing real-world scenes, generating edge-case scenarios, training policies, evaluating behavior and rapidly iterating. Today, these steps are fragmented across separate tools, slowing the pace of experimentation as researchers struggle to piece them together. Earlier this week, NVIDIA announced NVIDIA Cosmos 3, the open frontier model for physical AI and the world’s first full omnimodel unifying vision reasoning, world and action generation. Leading across the open model public leaderboards central to physical AI, the world foundation model provides core capabilities for physical AI development. NVIDIA physical AI skills pair with Cosmos, NVIDIA libraries and simulation frameworks to help researchers move from model capabilities to scalable end-to-end workflows faster than ever. Advancing Autonomous Vehicle Research Beyond Recorded Miles For AV researchers, the problem is the “long tail” of driving — rare interactions, unusual road geometry, lighting changes and edge-case behaviors that are difficult to repeatedly collect, but critical for training and validation. https://blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/06/NeuralReconstructionDemo.mp4 Neural Reconstruction skill demo in OpenClaw, showing a video re-rendered from an elevated virtual sensor viewpoint. With NVIDIA autonomous vehicle skills, researchers and developers can task AI agents to automate workflows for scene reconstruction from fleet data and generate synthetic scenarios. Neural Reconstruction skills help AI agents turn fleet-captured data into editable 3D scenes for simulation and synthetic data generation, while technologies including NVIDIA Omniverse NuRec, InstantNuRec, Harmonizer and HiGS accelerated renderer help accelerate reconstruction, improve scene realism and generate new views. https://blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/06/InstantNuRec.mp4 InstantNuRec enables fast 3D Gaussian road-scene reconstruction from images without per-scene optimization. For AV researchers, repeatable simulation helps vary conditions, compare system responses and uncover failure modes across scenarios beyond what can be captured in real-world data. NVIDIA AlpaGym, an open source closed-loop reinforcement learning framework, extends that approach by connecting policy rollouts and high-fidelity simulation with agent skills, scaling across thousands of GPUs, to help researchers move through setup, rollout and evaluation. NVIDIA OmniDreams, an action-conditioned generative world model, adds photorealistic rendering to the simulation loop, generating camera frames that respond directly to policy actions in real time. NVIDIA is also advancing AV research with its most powerful open driving foundation model to date: NVIDIA Alpamayo 2 Super, an open 32-billion-parameter reasoning vision language action (VLA) model that reasons, plans and acts across the full driving stack for safer, scalable level 4 development and deployment. Advancing Vision AI Systems for the Real World For vision AI research, the bottleneck is creating enough controlled examples to study how models behave when visual conditions, object states or temporal events change. Work in zero-shot anomaly detection, synthetic anomaly generation and few-shot defect recognition all run into the same data wall. https://blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/06/Delta-Defect-Image-Generation.mp4 New skills for visual inspection generates multiple rare defects on different surfaces. New NVIDIA Metropolis skills are helping researchers and developers use AI agents to generate synthetic visual scenarios, including anomalies, augment data and support pseudo-labeling. These skills benefit from Cosmos 3’s mixture-of-transformers architecture, which uses a reasoning transformer to analyze observations and feed instructions to a generation tower, helping scale physically grounded virtual worlds. Researchers building high-accuracy visual inspection models can use the Defect Image Generation skill to create examples of different defects across different surfaces using real images. The workflow combines NVIDIA Isaac Sim for simulation, Cosmos 3 and NVIDIA OSMO for orchestration and vision language reasoning — letting researchers create rare visual cases and assess whether models respond correctly. https://blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/06/VSS3_Demo.mp4 New NVIDIA Metropolis VSS Blueprint skills extract insights from massive volumes of video data. For video AI agents, the NVIDIA Metropolis Blueprint for video search and summarization (VSS), NVIDIA TAO and Video Augmentation skills help extract insights from massive volumes of video data, fine-tune models and automate the build-and-evaluate loop. This gives researchers a more repeatable way to develop reasoning vision AI agents that can detect events, reason over complex scenes, summarize activity and send alerts. Scaling Robot Learning With Agent-Ready Simulation Workflows Teaching robots skills like navigating or manipulating comes down to iteration. For researchers, the bottleneck is building enough controlled environments and policy rollouts to understand how robot behavior changes across tasks, settings and embodiments — work that typically means stitching together simulation environments, task variations, policy training and evaluation by hand. https://blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/06/Isaac-Sim.mp4 NVIDIA Isaac Sim 6.0 includes agent-friendly skills and connectors to help automate workflows. With NVIDIA robotics skills, researchers can task AI agents to automate most common development steps across scene preparation, simulation and robot learning with NVIDIA Omniverse libraries, Isaac Sim and Isaac Lab frameworks. Agents can help launch simulation sessions, author scenes, control simulation, capture data and validate environments in Isaac Sim, while Isaac Lab skills support reinforcement learning setup, training, evaluation and custom environment development. https://blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/06/COMPASS-grid.mp4 New NVIDIA Isaac mobility skills automate navigation workflows. Specialized skills extend that workflow to mobility and manipulation. Isaac mobility skills support navigation workflows spanning scene search, USD conversion, environment registration, residual reinforcement learning and policy evaluation, while specialized Isaac Lab agentic workflows help with sim-to-sim and sim-to-real tasks such as environment building, physics tuning, debugging and profiling. For healthcare robotics, Cosmos-H-Surgical-Simulator advances research by generating realistic surgical robotics data for policy training and evaluation. By learning directly from real surgical data rather than hand-engineered physics models, it helps reduce the sim-to-real gap, supporting the development of autonomous surgical tasks. Cosmos 3 can further help generate synthetic data and scene variations, then support post-training with embodiment-specific behavior and environment data for tasks ranging from pick-and-place to dexterous manipulation. NVIDIA Research at CVPR NVIDIA technologies — including GPUs, open models, simulation frameworks and CUDA-accelerated libraries — were referenced in the majority of accepted CVPR 2026 papers, with adoption across leading global research labs and institutions including Carnegie Mellon University, Stanford University, UC Berkeley, Tsinghua University and Peking University. NVIDIA researchers are presenting work across computer vision, physical AI, autonomous systems, neural rendering, generative AI and robotics at CVPR, running June 3-7 in Denver. NVIDIA’s CVPR presence also includes open research challenges that help benchmark progress in physical AI: The AI City Challenge, a premier computer vision competition for smart city applications now in its tenth year. The PAI-AV Reasoning Challenge, a new open benchmark evaluating how well VLA models explain driving decisions using chain-of-causation labels. The AlpaSim Closed-Loop End-to-End Driving Challenge, a new open benchmark testing autonomous driving policies in closed-loop simulation on real-world reconstructed scenarios. https://blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/06/RoboSim-Grid.mp4 Grid of samples videos from new Robot Sim Dataset as a part of Cosmos 3 dataset release. NVIDIA is also expanding the research infrastructure behind physical AI with datasets for training, fine-tuning and evaluation. The NVIDIA Physical AI Dataset has surpassed 15 million+ downloads on Hugging Face, while NVIDIA Isaac GR00T X Embodiment Sim has become one of the most-downloaded robotics datasets. New dataset releases include GRAIL, including roughly 50 hours of humanoid-object interaction data, and six synthetic video datasets used to train Cosmos 3 across robotics, physics, digital humans, autonomous driving, warehouse safety and spatial reasoning. Availability NVIDIA physical AI agent tools and skills are now openly available through GitHub. Agent skills and tools for synthetic data generation — Neural Reconstruction, Video Augmentation, Defect Image Generation — are also available to try instantly on NVIDIA Brev as Physical AI Launchables, preconfigured environments that bundle agent skills and tools for faster synthetic data generation and evaluation. Launchables run on hosted NVIDIA H100 Tensor Core GPUs and include free trial credits for researchers. Learn more about NVIDIA at CVPR and explore NVIDIA Research’s work in physical AI, computer vision and autonomous systems. Get started with Isaac GR00T and NVIDIA robotics tools.

history.titleZh: NVIDIA以智能体技能赋能自动驾驶、机器人和视觉AI，开启物理AI研究新时代
history.descriptionZh:
在CVPR 2026大会上，NVIDIA发布了一系列全新的物理AI代理技能，旨在帮助研究人员和开发者加速自动驾驶、机器人以及视觉AI系统的研发进程。当前物理AI研究的核心挑战并非单纯构建更强大的模型，而是围绕模型打造完整的工作流程——包括重建真实世界场景、生成边缘案例场景、训练策略、评估行为以及快速迭代。目前，这些步骤分散在独立的工具中，研究人员需要费力拼凑，从而拖慢了实验节奏。

本周早些时候，NVIDIA宣布推出NVIDIA Cosmos 3——面向物理AI的开放前沿模型，也是世界上首个完整的全模态模型（omnimodel），统一了视觉推理、世界生成与动作生成。该世界基础模型在物理AI领域的主流开放模型排行榜上位居前列，为物理AI开发提供了核心能力。NVIDIA的物理AI技能与Cosmos、NVIDIA库以及仿真框架相结合，帮助研究人员从模型能力更快地过渡到可扩展的端到端工作流。

### 推动自动驾驶研究突破“记录里程”限制

对于自动驾驶研究人员而言，核心问题在于驾驶的“长尾”——罕见交互、异常道路几何形状、光照变化以及边界行为。这些场景难以重复采集，但对于训练和验证至关重要。借助NVIDIA的自动驾驶技能，研究人员和开发者可以任务化AI代理，实现从车队数据进行场景重建并生成合成场景的自动化工作流。

- **神经重建（Neural Reconstruction）技能**：帮助AI代理将车队采集的数据转化为可编辑的3D场景，用于仿真和合成数据生成。涉及的底层技术包括NVIDIA Omniverse NuRec、InstantNuRec、Harmonizer以及HiGS加速渲染器，它们加速了重建过程，提升了场景真实感并能够生成新视角。
- **InstantNuRec**：支持从图像中快速进行3D高斯道路场景重建，无需针对每个场景进行优化。
- **可重复仿真**：对于自动驾驶研究，可重复的仿真有助于改变条件、比较系统响应，并发现超出真实数据采集范围的故障模式。NVIDIA AlpaGym作为开源闭环强化学习框架，通过将策略 rollout 与高保真仿真以及代理技能相连接，可扩展至数千GPU，帮助研究人员快速完成设置、运行和评估。
- **NVIDIA OmniDreams**：一种动作条件生成世界模型，为仿真循环添加了逼真渲染，能够实时生成直接响应策略动作的相机帧。
- **NVIDIA Alpamayo 2 Super**：NVIDIA迄今为止最强大的开放驾驶基础模型，是一个拥有320亿参数的推理视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型，能够在整个驾驶堆栈中执行推理、规划与动作，为更安全的可扩展L4级开发与部署提供支持。

### 推进面向真实世界的视觉AI系统

在视觉AI研究中，瓶颈在于创建足够多的受控样本，以研究模型在视觉条件、物体状态或时间事件变化时的行为。零样本异常检测、合成异常生成以及少样本缺陷识别等工作都面临着相同的数据墙。

NVIDIA新的Metropolis技能帮助研究人员和开发者利用AI代理生成合成视觉场景（包括异常情况）、扩充数据并支持伪标签。这些技能受益于Cosmos 3的混合变换器架构（mixture-of-transformers），该架构使用推理变换器分析观察结果，并向生成塔（generation tower）提供指令，从而帮助扩展物理基础的虚拟世界。

- **缺陷图像生成（Defect Image Generation）技能**：研究人员可以利用该技能，基于真实图像在不同表面上生成不同缺陷的样本。工作流结合了NVIDIA Isaac Sim（仿真）、Cosmos 3、NVIDIA OSMO（编排）以及视觉语言推理，让研究人员可以创建罕见视觉案例并评估模型是否正确响应。
- **NVIDIA Metropolis视频搜索与摘要蓝图（VSS）技能**：以及NVIDIA TAO和视频增强技能，帮助从海量视频数据中提取洞察，微调模型，并自动化构建与评估循环。这为研究人员提供了一种更可重复的方法来开发推理型视觉AI代理，使其能够检测事件、推理复杂场景、总结活动并发送警报。

### 扩展机器人学习：代理就绪的仿真工作流

训练机器人执行导航或操作等技能，本质上是迭代过程。研究人员的瓶颈在于构建足够多的受控环境和策略运行，以理解机器人行为如何随任务、设置和具体形态而变化——这通常需要手动拼接仿真环境、任务变体、策略训练和评估。

借助NVIDIA的机器人技能，研究人员可以任务化AI代理，利用NVIDIA Omniverse库、Isaac Sim和Isaac Lab框架，自动化场景准备、仿真和机器人学习中最常见的开发步骤。AI代理可以启动仿真会话、创建场景、控制仿真、捕获数据并在Isaac Sim中验证环境，而Isaac Lab技能则支持强化学习设置、训练、评估和自定义环境开发。

- **Isaac移动技能（Mobility Skills）**：支持导航工作流，涵盖场景搜索、USD转换、环境注册、残差强化学习和策略评估。专门的Isaac Lab代理工作流则帮助完成 sim-to-sim 和 sim-to-real 任务，如环境构建、物理调优、调试和分析。
- **Cosmos-H-手术仿真器（Cosmos-H-Surgical-Simulator）**：通过生成真实手术机器人数据用于策略训练和评估，推进医疗机器人研究。该仿真器直接从真实手术数据学习，而非依赖手工设计的物理模型，有助于缩小 sim-to-real 差距，支持自主手术任务的开发。
- **Cosmos 3的通用能力**：可以进一步帮助生成合成数据和场景变体，然后支持使用特定形态行为和环境数据进行后训练，涵盖从抓取放置到灵巧操作等任务。

### NVIDIA在CVPR上的研究贡献

NVIDIA的技术——包括GPU、开放模型、仿真框架和CUDA加速库——被大部分CVPR 2026录用论文所引用，并被全球顶尖研究实验室和机构（如卡内基梅隆大学、斯坦福大学、加州大学伯克利分校、清华大学和北京大学）广泛采用。NVIDIA研究人员在6月3日至7日于丹佛举行的CVPR上，展示了计算机视觉、物理AI、自主系统、神经渲染、生成式AI和机器人学等领域的工作。

NVIDIA还在CVPR上举办了多项开放研究挑战赛，以推动物理AI领域的基准测试：
- **AI City Challenge**：面向智慧城市应用的首届计算机视觉竞赛，今年已是第十届。
- **PAI-AV推理挑战**：一个新的开放基准，用于评估VLA模型如何利用因果关系标签解释驾驶决策。
- **AlpaSim闭环端到端驾驶挑战**：一个新的开放基准，在真实重建场景的闭环仿真中测试自动驾驶策略。

此外，NVIDIA正在通过数据集扩展物理AI的研究基础设施。NVIDIA物理AI数据集在Hugging Face上的下载量已超过1500万次，而NVIDIA Isaac GR00T X具身仿真数据集已成为下载量最高的机器人数据集之一。新发布的数据集包括GRAIL（约50小时的人形物体交互数据），以及用于训练Cosmos 3的六个合成视频数据集，涵盖机器人学、物理、数字人、自动驾驶、仓库安全与空间推理等领域。

### 可用性

NVIDIA的物理AI代理工具和技能现已在GitHub上开放。用于合成数据生成的代理技能和工具——如神经重建、视频增强、缺陷图像生成——也可通过NVIDIA Brev平台作为“物理AI启动包”（Physical AI Launchables）进行即时试用，这些预配置环境捆绑了代理技能和工具，可加速合成数据生成与评估。启动包运行在托管的NVIDIA H100 Tensor Core GPU上，并为研究人员提供免费试用积分。更多信息可访问NVIDIA在CVPR的页面，以及Isaac GR00T和NVIDIA机器人工具集。

history.title: Microsoft unveils Project Solara AI, a chip-to-cloud platform built to power a new generation of 'agent-first' enterprise devices — hardware designed to run AI agents instead of traditional apps
history.description: Microsoft has unveiled Project Solara, an Android-based chip-to-cloud platform for AI-first enterprise devices. The system combines Qualcomm and MediaTek hardware, Azure-hosted agents, and adaptive interfaces, with reference designs including a wearable AI badge and a desktop AI hub.
history.content: Share Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Microsoft has unveiled Project Solara, a chip-to-cloud platform designed to power a new generation of "agent-first" enterprise devices — hardware designed to run AI agents instead of traditional apps. Announced at the Microsoft Build 2026 Developer Conference on the 2nd of June, the platform, developed by Microsoft's Applied Sciences Group, features a lightweight edge OS called the Microsoft Device Ecosystem Platform (MDEP). Interestingly, the OS is built on the Android Open Source Project (AOSP) rather than Windows.MDEP is paired with Azure-hosted agent services and persistent cloud-based state, meaning devices act as interfaces to AI agents running across Microsoft's cloud infrastructure rather than as fully self-contained computers. Together, the software stack forms what Microsoft describes as a chip-to-cloud architecture for enterprise AI devices, combining cloud-hosted agents with centralized security, management, and orchestration capabilities."The 'operating system' is liminal, transcending the device and the cloud. The system brings a lightweight window to the edge, where the agent manifests and where the state, via Azure, can encompass a constellation of specialized devices,” explained Steven Bathiche, Microsoft's Corporate Vice President and Technical Fellow in the Applied Sciences Group.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: To populate that ecosystem with hardware, Microsoft has partnered with Qualcomm and MediaTek as its first silicon partners — Qualcomm for portable and wearable form factors and MediaTek for stationary devices. The company has no plans to manufacture end products itself. Instead, the company is releasing reference designs for OEMs to build from, alongside an "approved chipsets" requirement that gives Microsoft certification-level control over which hardware qualifies for the platform, similar to Google's GMS certification model for Android.To demonstrate Solara, Microsoft unveiled two concept reference designs built on the platform. A stationary desk-mounted AI hub built around MediaTek IoT silicon and a wearable AI badge powered by Qualcomm hardware. The desktop companion features a display, a camera, a UWB (ultra-wideband) presence sensor that handles automatic login and lock, dual far-field mics, and two USB-C ports. Connected to an external display, the device can double as a Windows 365 cloud PC client. You may like Nvidia unveils RTX Spark Superchip for laptops and desktop PCs at Computex 2026 Microsoft debuts Surface RTX Spark Dev Box — Nvidia-powered mini-PC helps devs get ready for an agentic Windows Nvidia reportedly building its own AI agent to compete with OpenClaw, report claims Meanwhile, the wearable badge is equipped with a touchscreen, Hello for Business fingerprint sensor, far-field high-SNR microphone array, side-facing camera, and 5G, WiFi, Bluetooth, and GNSS connectivity — targeting front-line workers such as nurses, retail staff, and field workers. Microsoft confirmed that both devices are intended as reference designs for OEM partners rather than retail products. Project Solara: A new vision for agent-first computing - YouTube Watch On Central to the platform is what Microsoft calls just-in-time UI — an adaptive interface layer that allows a single agent to render appropriately across different screen sizes and input modalities without requiring developers to rebuild the experience for each device. Microsoft positions this on a spectrum between conventional responsive design and fully generative UI, where AI constructs interfaces dynamically with no predefined structure; Solara currently targets the middle ground, prioritizing consistency while avoiding per-device redesign overhead.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors“The same agent can render a custom experience on multiple screen sizes and modalities with little or no additional work from the developer. For us, that is the first proof point: a path to specialized devices without requiring developers to rebuild the experience from scratch each time,” said Bathiche.Another notable aspect of Solara is Microsoft's decision to build MDEP on Android rather than Windows. AOSP scales naturally to the lightweight, constrained hardware that wearables and embedded devices run on — something Windows, with its memory and processing overhead, was never designed to do. It also sidesteps the application compatibility expectations that come with Windows. Because Solara devices are built around cloud-hosted agents rather than traditional software, Microsoft can optimize the platform for dedicated AI hardware without carrying decades of legacy PC baggage.To manage multiple agents running simultaneously, Microsoft is also working on an agent dispatcher and agent task manager — components that automatically surface or activate the right agent based on context, rather than requiring users to launch each one manually. Neither component is shipping yet. Early agent integrations include Dragon Copilot for healthcare workflows and GitHub Copilot for developer task tracking — both exploring how persistent, context-aware agents behave differently on dedicated hardware than they do inside a browser or IDE.The platform appears to be aimed at enterprise buyers in retail, healthcare, and field service sectors, where dedicated agent hardware makes more sense than repurposing a smartphone. Microsoft has already lined up pilots with Best Buy, CVS Health, Levi's, Target, and AccuWeather, with broader OEM deployment targeted across healthcare, hospitality, financial services, legal, and industrial verticals. history.author: Etiido Uko
history.titleZh: 微软推出Project Solara AI，这是一个芯片到云平台，旨在驱动新一代“智能体优先”的企业设备——专为运行AI智能体而非传统应用而设计的硬件
history.descriptionZh:
微软在2026年6月2日的Build开发者大会上正式发布了Project Solara，这是一个全新的“芯片到云”平台，旨在驱动新一代“智能体优先”（agent-first）的企业设备。与当前以传统应用为核心的设备不同，Solara是专门为运行AI智能体而设计的硬件平台，由微软应用科学集团开发。其核心软件层是一个名为Microsoft Device Ecosystem Platform（MDEP）的轻量级边缘操作系统，有趣的是，MDEP并非基于Windows，而是构建在Android开源项目（AOSP）之上。MDEP与Azure托管的智能体服务及持久化的云端状态紧密结合，使得设备本身更像是一个与微软云基础设施中运行的AI智能体进行交互的界面，而非完全自成一体的计算机。微软将该软件栈描述为一种企业AI设备的“芯片到云”架构，融合了云端智能体与集中化的安全、管理和编排能力。微软应用科学集团企业副总裁兼技术院士Steven Bathiche解释说，这个“操作系统”是阈限的，跨越了设备和云；它给边缘带来一个轻量级的窗口，智能体在此显现，而状态则通过Azure可以涵盖一组专门化的设备。

为了构建硬件生态系统，微软已选择高通和联发科作为首批芯片合作伙伴——高通负责便携式和可穿戴设备形态，联发科负责桌面式设备。微软本身并不计划生产终端产品，而是发布参考设计供OEM厂商自行制造，并引入“经批准芯片组”要求，从而获得类似谷歌GMS认证的管控级别，确保只有符合平台标准的硬件才能使用Solara。作为演示，微软推出了两个概念参考设计：一个是基于联发科物联网芯片的桌面式AI集线器，配备显示屏、摄像头、UWB（超宽带）存在传感器（可自动登录/锁定）、双远场麦克风和两个USB-C端口，连接到外部显示器后可兼作Windows 365云电脑客户端；另一个是基于高通硬件的可穿戴AI徽章，配备触摸屏、Windows Hello for Business指纹传感器、远场高信噪比麦克风阵列、侧置摄像头，并支持5G、WiFi、蓝牙和GNSS连接，主要面向护士、零售人员、外勤人员等一线工作者。微软确认这两款设备均为面向OEM合作伙伴的参考设计，而非零售产品。

Solara平台的核心创新在于微软所谓的“即时用户界面”（just-in-time UI）——一个自适应的界面层，允许同一个智能体在不同屏幕尺寸和输入模态上呈现合适的体验，而无需开发者为每个设备重新构建界面。微软将此技术定位在传统响应式设计与完全生成式UI之间，目前专注于中间地带：优先保证一致性，同时避免为每种设备单独重新设计的负担。Bathiche强调：“同一个智能体可以在多种屏幕尺寸和模态上渲染出定制化体验，开发者几乎不需要额外工作。对我们来说，这是第一个证明点：一条通往专门化设备的路径，而不要求开发者每次都从头构建体验。”

另一个值得注意的方面是微软选择基于Android而非Windows构建MDEP。AOSP天然适用于可穿戴设备和嵌入式设备所需的轻量级、受限硬件——Windows由于内存和处理开销而从未为此设计过。这也避免了Windows带来的应用兼容性预期。由于Solara设备围绕云端智能体而非传统软件，微软可以针对专用AI硬件优化平台，而无需背负数十年的PC遗留包袱。为了管理同时运行的多个智能体，微软还在开发智能体调度器（agent dispatcher）和智能体任务管理器（agent task manager），它们能根据上下文自动调出或激活正确的智能体，而非要求用户手动启动每一个。目前这两项组件尚未发货。早期的智能体集成包括面向医疗工作流的Dragon Copilot和面向开发者任务跟踪的GitHub Copilot，两者都在探索持久化、上下文感知的智能体在专用硬件上如何展现出与在浏览器或IDE中不同的行为。

该平台显然瞄准企业买家，尤其是在零售、医疗和现场服务领域，因为在这些场景中，专用智能体硬件比改造智能手机更有意义。微软已经与百思买、CVS Health、Levi's、Target和AccuWeather等公司启动了试点项目，并计划将OEM部署扩展到医疗、酒店、金融服务、法律和工业等垂直行业。整体来看，Project Solara代表了一种全新的计算范式：设备不再是应用的载体，而是AI智能体的物理入口，云端则成为智能体的真正家园。

history.title: Trump signs AI executive order seeking 30-day government access to frontier models before release — voluntary framework will include classified benchmark to determine which models qualify
history.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order on Tuesday that asks AI companies to give the federal government early access to their most capable models.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter President Donald Trump signed an executive order on Tuesday that asks AI companies to give the federal government early access to their most capable models for up to 30 days before any wider release, a window trimmed from the 90 days written into a draft he walked away from last month.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowWhile it’s a voluntary framework that creates no licensing or pre-clearance requirement, it tasks the National Security Agency, the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA), and the National Institute of Standards and Technology with building a classified benchmark that decides which systems qualify as a "covered frontier model."The 30-day review only applies to models that meet the classified threshold, which the NSA will set in consultation with the National Cyber Director and CISA. Developers who opt in would open their models to agency review before the systems reach other trusted partners, and the order puts those same agencies in the room to help choose the partners. Section three states that nothing in it authorizes mandatory government licensing, pre-clearance, or permitting for new models, including frontier models.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: The order also directs the Treasury Department to stand up an "AI cybersecurity clearinghouse" that coordinates vulnerability scanning, validation, and patch distribution alongside AI firms and operators of critical infrastructure such as rural hospitals, community banks, and local utilities. Separate provisions steer federal grant money toward companies building AI vulnerability detection and widen the U.S. Tech Force's cybersecurity hiring pathways.In April, Anthropic's Claude Mythos preview identified thousands of zero-day vulnerabilities across major operating systems and web browsers, and the company has since grown its Project Glasswing early-access program for the model. It’s clear that the order has come in response to Mythos and other increasingly powerful frontier models, with reports suggesting that the Trump administration had been weighing pre-release vetting since at least the spring, when officials briefed Anthropic, Google, and OpenAI on an order that an early, unsigned draft would have made mandatory. You may like White House reportedly considers mandatory government vetting of AI models before release Trump administration considers mandatory pre-release vetting of AI models — Anthropic's Mythos cited as catalyst for policy reversal Google, Microsoft, and xAI agree to let US government test AI models before public release The trusted-partner provision of the order is likely to draw extensive criticism because ot places the government in the room when labs decide who gets first access to their strongest models. Speaking to The Register, Cato Institute policy analyst Juan Londoño said that such moves could “open the door to potential weaponization against companies that have any sort of conflict with the administration.”This isn’t a hypothetical, either; the Department of Defense labeled Anthropic a supply chain risk shortly before the Mythos preview shipped, a designation that bars defense contractors from using the company's technology, and Anthropic has sued to overturn it. That litigation remains unresolved.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.

history.titleZh: 特朗普签署AI行政令，要求前沿模型发布前30天向政府开放——自愿框架将纳入机密基准以判定模型资格
history.descriptionZh:
美国总统特朗普于周二签署了一项行政令，要求人工智能公司在向公众广泛发布其最先进模型之前，提前最多30天向联邦政府提供早期访问权限。这一期限较上个月他放弃的草案中规定的90天窗口期有所缩短。该行政令属于自愿框架，没有设立许可证或预审批要求，但指示国家安全局（NSA）、网络安全和基础设施安全局（CISA）以及国家标准与技术研究院（NIST）共同建立一套机密基准，用以判断哪些系统属于“受覆盖前沿模型”。只有达到该机密阈值的模型才适用30天审查，阈值将由NSA在与国家网络总监和CISA协商后设定。选择加入的开发者需在模型交付给其他受信任合作伙伴之前，开放供上述机构审查；同时，这些机构也将参与决定哪些合作伙伴可以优先获得模型。行政令第三节明确指出，任何内容均不授权政府对包括前沿模型在内的新模型实施强制性的许可、预审批或许可制度。

此外，行政令还要求财政部建立“人工智能网络安全信息交换中心”，与人工智能公司以及关键基础设施运营商（如农村医院、社区银行和本地公用事业）协调漏洞扫描、验证和补丁分发工作。其他条款则引导联邦拨款流向开发人工智能漏洞检测的公司，并拓宽美国技术力量（U.S. Tech Force）的网络安全招聘渠道。今年4月，Anthropic公司的Claude Mythos预览版在主流操作系统和网页浏览器中发现了数千个零日漏洞，此后该公司扩大了该模型的Project Glasswing早期访问计划。显然，该行政令是对Mythos及其他日益强大的前沿模型的回应。有报道称，至少从春季开始，特朗普政府就一直在权衡预发布审查事宜，当时官员们曾向Anthropic、谷歌和OpenAI通报了一份早期未签署的草案，该草案曾试图将审查变为强制性要求。

行政令中的“受信任合作伙伴”条款可能招致广泛批评，因为它让政府参与决定实验室谁能够优先获得其最强大的模型。卡托研究所政策分析师胡安·隆多尼奥在接受The Register采访时表示，此类举措可能“为针对任何与政府存在冲突的公司进行潜在武器化打开大门”。这并非假设：在Mythos预览版发布前不久，美国国防部将Anthropic列为供应链风险，从而禁止国防承包商使用其技术；Anthropic已提起诉讼试图推翻这一决定，该诉讼目前仍未解决。

history.title: AMD says new EXPO ‘Ultra Low Latency’ DDR5 memory should be 'effectively the same price' as current kits — feature will work on existing chipsets, but will require new DIMMs
history.description: AMD provides us with a bit more detail about its upcoming EXPO Ultra Low Latency mode, which should be available from leading memory partners soon.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter AMD announced EXPO Ultra Low Latency at Computex 2026, but provided very few details about what it is or how it works. The company claims it provides a 13% average uplift compared to JEDEC standards, and a 4% uplift compared to standard EXPO, and that’s it’s coming from leading memory partners. Now, AMD’s David McAfee, VP and general manager of Ryzen and Radeon, has given Tom’s Hardware a bit more color on what exactly EXPO ULL is and how it will roll out.“Expo has evolved, and we've been working with all of the module vendors. We've seen an opportunity with adding more sub timings to the SPD profile, and just getting a little bit lower latency,” McAfee told Tom’s Hardware. Beyond what EXPO ULL is doing, AMD says that it will work with existing chipsets and motherboards, but McAfee clarified that he wasn’t sure if it would require a BIOS update (that seems likely). Regardless, McAfee said: “My advice to all of your readers and viewers is: update your bios.”EXPO ULL kits will come with different branding on them, including a new badge, so “it’ll be noticeable that these kits are different.” McAfee also says that, although AMD doesn’t control memory or module prices, “[AMD’s] understanding from the partners is [that] they expect to bring these in at effectively the same price points that the current kits are at.”Those price points, at the moment, are inflated due to ongoing memory shortages. But McAfee isn’t being coy here about pricing. The executive says ULL is “simply about extending the sub-timings and really getting every little bit of OC performance out of those DIMMs,” so there’s nothing specifically about ULL that would cause the DIMMs to be radically more expensive. You may like AMD promises 13% uplift with new EXPO ‘Ultra Low Latency’ overclocking on DDR5 DIMMs AMD's memory-boosting EXPO 1.2 is here, adds support for three Chinese memory vendors EXPO 1.2 will come to 600-series AM5 motherboards later, tech only enables partial CUDIMM support (Image credit: AMD)Across a test suite of 30 games with the Ryzen 7 9700X, AMD says ULL delivered a 4% uplift compared to standard EXPO, with both running at DDR5-6000, and a 13% uplift compared to JEDEC-standard DDR5-5600. 1% lows, as expected, see more of an improvement, with a 15% uplift compared to JEDEC and the same 4% compared to standard EXPO.AMD says that EXPO ULL is “coming soon,” but it hasn’t said anything beyond that for a release window. Regardless, it already has partners lined up to support the feature, including G.Skill, Kingston, Lexar, XPG, and TeamGroup.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Jake Roach
history.titleZh: AMD表示新版支持EXPO的“超低延迟”DDR5内存将“与现有套件价格基本持平”——此功能可在现有芯片组上工作，但需使用新DIMMs
history.descriptionZh:
AMD在Computex 2026上宣布了EXPO Ultra Low Latency（EXPO ULL）技术，但当时并未透露具体细节。现在，AMD的Ryzen和Radeon副总裁兼总经理David McAfee在接受Tom's Hardware采访时，详细解释了这项技术的原理、性能提升、兼容性以及市场策略。

EXPO ULL本质上是AMD针对DDR5内存的EXPO超频技术的进一步优化。其核心创新在于：在内存的SPD（串行存在检测）配置文件中，加入了更多、更精细的子时序（sub-timings）参数。通过这些额外的时序微调，系统能够在保持同等频率的前提下，显著降低内存访问延迟。McAfee表示：“EXPO一直在进化，我们一直在与所有内存模组厂商合作。我们发现，在SPD配置文件中增加更多子时序，可以进一步降低延迟。”这意味着EXPO ULL并非通过提高频率来获取性能，而是通过更精细的时序控制来“压榨”出每一丝延迟优化潜力。

在性能参数方面，AMD提供了明确的对比数据：在30款游戏的测试套件中，使用Ryzen 7 9700X处理器，当内存均运行在DDR5-6000频率时，EXPO ULL相比标准EXPO平均帧率提升4%，而相比JEDEC标准的DDR5-5600提升13%。更值得关注的是1%低帧（Lows）表现，这直接反映了游戏卡顿和流畅度。EXPO ULL的1%低帧相比JEDEC提升了15%，相比标准EXPO同样提升4%。这说明延迟降低在压力场景下（如复杂战斗、快速场景切换）能带来更稳定的帧率体验。

兼容性方面，McAfee确认EXPO ULL将兼容现有AM5芯片组和主板（包括600系列和800系列），但很可能需要更新BIOS才能启用。他明确建议所有用户“更新你的BIOS”。此外，带有EXPO ULL认证的内存套件将采用全新的品牌标识和徽章，方便消费者识别。关于价格，尽管当前内存市场因短缺导致价格偏高，但McAfee强调，EXPO ULL并非增加成本的昂贵方案：“这本质上只是扩展了子时序，从这些DIMM中榨出每一丝超频性能。”因此，合作伙伴预计会将EXPO ULL内存以与现有标准EXPO套件相近的定价推向市场。

目前，AMD已确认数家顶级内存厂商将支持EXPO ULL，包括芝奇（G.Skill）、金士顿（Kingston）、雷克沙（Lexar）、威刚旗下的XPG以及十铨（TeamGroup）。AMD表示EXPO ULL“即将到来”，但未公布具体上市时间窗口。

技术影响和应用场景方面：EXPO ULL为DDR5内存用户提供了一条低门槛的性能提升路径——无需更换主板、无需购买高频率内存，只需选择带有新徽标的优化套件并更新BIOS，即可在游戏和响应敏感的应用中获得额外性能。这对于追求极致游戏帧率稳定性的PC发烧友和电竞玩家尤为有价值。同时，它也展示了AMD与内存生态链深度合作、通过固件级优化释放硬件潜力的思路，而非单纯依赖更高频率的硬件迭代。

history.title: Astera Labs showcases 320-lane PCIe 6.0 switch for vendor-agnostic scaling in data centers — up to 80 accelerators can be scaled up using PCIe alone
history.description: Astera Labs has shown off the Scorpio X-Series 320-lane PCIe switch that promises to enable vendor-agnostic scale-up capability for AI infrastructure and disaggregated data center infrastructure.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Astera Labs demonstrated its recently introduced Scorpio X-Series 320 Lane Smart Fabric Switch, which appears to be the industry’s largest open memory-semantic fabric switch, at Computex 2026 in Taipei. The PCIe 6.0 switch with 320 lanes can be used to build large multi-GPU scale-up clusters, large shared KV-cache memory pools, and disaggregate data center infrastructure using custom topologies.Image 1 of 3(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The switch provides 320 PCIe 6.0 lanes and 20 Tbps of switching bandwidth, up from 144 lanes and 9 Tbps for previous-generation devices. Astera Labs says the increased number of lanes enables larger scale-up domains, enabling the connection of up to 80 accelerators using a single switch. By contrast, older 144-lane switches support up to 32 accelerators per switch. For clusters with more than 64 accelerators, the company says the new device reduces switch hops from as many as three to one and cuts switch count by a factor of four to six while still providing all-to-all connectivity akin to that provided by Nvidia’s NVL72 systems (albeit with lower bandwidth and higher latencies). The switch can support both standard and custom accelerators as long as they use standard PCIe connectivity. (Image credit: Astera Labs)On the trade show floor, Astera Labs is showing off its switching capabilities with Intel’s Arc B70 Pro graphics cards. However, real-world deployments based on the Scorpio X-Series 320-lane PCIe switches will likely use more advanced Intel hardware. In general, the switch can be used to build clusters from all types of accelerators that do not support their own NVLink or UALink-like interconnections, including AMD’s Instinct MI350P and Nvidia’s RTX 6000 Blackwell. Astera has yet to showcase a full working cluster featuring 80 accelerators, as the company only got the Scorpio X-Series 320-lane PCIe switch from the fab eight weeks ago. Also, finding 80 similar accelerators is not easy. Nonetheless, based on the company’s demonstration, the switch appears to be working.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)A key feature of the Scorpio X-Series is Hypercast, which is a hardware-based data replication engine intended to accelerate communication-intensive operations common in AI models. According to Astera Labs, MoE networks tend to route tokens across hundreds of experts and create large amounts of multicast traffic between accelerators. In such cases, traditional switching architectures either require repeated data transmissions or slow multicast-group reconfiguration, whereas Hypercast is designed to handle these communication patterns directly in hardware, reduce GPU networking overhead, and improve accelerator efficiency, Astera Labs claims. (Image credit: Astera Labs)The company also added In-Network Compute engines that offload collective operations such as AllReduce, ReduceScatter, AllGather, AllScatter, and all-to-all exchanges. These features can reduce communication latency by more than 50% in certain workloads, according to Astera. You may like Phison shows PCIe 6.0 X3 SSD controller with 28 GB/s of bandwidth and 6.8 million IOPS, supports 2 petabytes per drive TSMC's details next-gen CoWoS roadmap: over 14-reticle packages and 48x leap in compute power expected by 2029 Tech titans team up to form optical interconnect alliance to solve the AI buildout's big data bottleneck Another important feature of the Scorpio X-Series 320-lane PCIe switch is its memory-semantic connectivity, which enables connected processors to access fabric-attached resources using native load and store operations rather than software-controlled transactions. This greatly simplifies usage of the device and improves real-world performance by reducing overhead and improving fabric efficiency at scale. (Image credit: Astera Labs)Astera says that the production ramp of the Scorpio X-Series 320-lane PCIe switch is set for the second half of 2026. Currently, the company is sampling the switch with leading hyperscalers.
history.author: Anton Shilov
history.titleZh: Astera Labs 展示320通道PCIe 6.0交换机，实现数据中心供应商无关的扩展——仅使用PCIe即可扩展多达80个加速器
history.descriptionZh:
Astera Labs在2026年台北Computex展会上展示了其最新推出的Scorpio X-Series 320通道智能织物交换机，这款产品被认为是业界规模最大的开放内存语义织物交换机。该交换机基于PCIe 6.0协议，提供320条通道，总交换带宽高达20 Tbps，相比前一代的144通道和9 Tbps有了显著提升。这一升级使得单个交换机能够连接多达80个加速器，而之前的144通道交换机仅支持每个交换机连接32个加速器。对于超过64个加速器的集群，新交换机可将交换跳数从最多三次减少到一次，并将所需的交换机数量减少4到6倍，同时仍能提供类似Nvidia NVL72系统的全互联拓扑（尽管带宽较低、延迟略高）。该交换机支持使用标准PCIe连接的任何标准或定制加速器。

在展会现场，Astera Labs使用Intel Arc B70 Pro显卡展示了交换能力，但实际部署中更可能采用更先进的Intel硬件。总体而言，该交换机可用于构建由各种加速器组成的集群，包括AMD Instinct MI350P和Nvidia RTX 6000 Blackwell等不支持NVLink或UALink类互连的加速器。由于Astera Labs仅在八周前从晶圆厂拿到Scorpio X-Series 320通道PCIe交换机，目前尚未展示完整的80加速器集群，但基于演示，交换机工作正常。

Scorpio X-Series的关键特性之一是Hypercast，这是一种基于硬件的数据复制引擎，旨在加速AI模型中常见的通信密集型操作。据Astera Labs介绍，混合专家（MoE）网络通常需要将令牌路由到数百个专家节点，并在加速器之间产生大量多播流量。传统交换架构要么需要重复数据传输，要么多播组重配置缓慢，而Hypercast直接在硬件中处理这些通信模式，减少GPU网络开销，提高加速器效率。

此外，该交换机集成了网络内计算引擎，可卸载AllReduce、ReduceScatter、AllGather、AllScatter和全对全交换等集合操作。据Astera宣称，这些特性可将某些工作负载的通信延迟降低超过50%。另一个重要特性是内存语义连接，使连接的处理器能够通过原生加载和存储操作访问织物附接的资源，而无需软件控制的事务处理，从而简化使用并提升实际性能，降低开销并提高大规模织物效率。

Astera Labs计划于2026年下半年开始量产Scorpio X-Series 320通道PCIe交换机，目前正在向主要超大规模云服务商提供样品。

history.title: AI has a water problem — Google thinks it has a fix
history.description:
In the face of widespread backlash to the AI data center buildout throughout the US, Google is touting its efforts to minimize the environmental impact by actually increasing water for local communities. 
The company laid out five commitments around water use in a new blog post published Wednesday, including a goal to replenish more water than it uses at its data centers by 2030. Google also said it will invest in local water infrastructure, identify alternative water sources to power its facilities, and be transparent about its water use overall. 
"We're just one of dozens of players in the space," Google's global head of infrastructure a …
Read the full story at The Verge.
history.content: In the face of widespread backlash to the AI data center buildout throughout the US, Google is touting its efforts to minimize the environmental impact by actually increasing water for local communities.The company laid out five commitments around water use in a new blog post published Wednesday, including a goal to replenish more water than it uses at its data centers by 2030. Google also said it will invest in local water infrastructure, identify alternative water sources to power its facilities, and be transparent about its water use overall.“We’re just one of dozens of players in the space,” Google’s global head of infrastructure and sustainability Ben Townsend told The Verge in an interview. “We think it’s really important to sort of put a blueprint out there that communities can reference, so if somebody else comes and says, ‘we’d like to build a data center there,’ a community can say, ‘well, here are five different things that really put the community and the watershed first. Are you doing these? Are you doing one of them? All of them? None of them? And if not, why?’”The commitments come amid growing opposition to the rapid data center buildout helping to power the extensive energy needs of AI. Google parent company Alphabet recently said it wants to raise $80 billion from stock sales to fund its buildout for the technology. A recent Gallup poll found that more than 70 percent of Americans oppose the idea of a data center being built in their area. Half of the respondents cited data centers’ impact on environmental resources as a motivator behind their opposition, including 18 percent who cited excess water use as an issue.A recent Gallup poll found that more than 70 percent of Americans oppose the idea of a data center being built in their areaAI data centers require vast amounts of water for cooling, with a recent study finding that the technology used as much water annually as people drink from water bottles worldwide. Google’s prior estimates of its own water use for AI have been misleading, according to some researchers, who say they omit indirect water usage. The widespread resource concerns have driven a wave of commitments across the industry to limit water usage and prevent data centers from driving up consumer energy prices.But Google’s Townsend defended the company’s record thus far. “To the best of our ability, we are accounting for the offsite water footprint,” Townsend said, “and I think we’ve made significant progress in reducing or eliminating the water footprint of that supply chain through our waterless renewable energy investments.”In the blog post, Google’s vice president of global infrastructure Bikash Koley says water usage at data centers can lower overall energy use. “In many places, water cooling can reduce data center energy use by approximately 10% compared to air cooling,” Koley writes. “The aggregate water consumption of data centers is small — U.S. data centers use less than 1% of the water that Americans use on their lawns annually — but we are focused on protecting local water resources in all aspects of our data center operations.”Koley says Google will be able to replenish more water than it consumes in the next four years by investing in projects that improve things like irrigation and infrastructure. It promises to keep reporting its annual water use and look for alternative sources like reclaimed wastewater, as it’s done in one Georgia county. The company also announced $17 million to support new water stewardship projects across seven states.Those who worry about data centers sucking up all their water have valid concerns, Townsend said, though he says they might use less water than people think. “It would be a real disservice to the space to say there’s only misconceptions out there. That’s not true,” Townsend said. But, he added, the data center sector doesn’t use as much water as people might think, and now is the time to invest to make sure “that data center water use doesn’t become a problem.”
history.author: Lauren Feiner
history.titleZh: AI面临水资源问题——谷歌认为已有对策
history.descriptionZh:
在美国，AI数据中心的快速扩张正引发广泛反对，谷歌近日发布新博客，详细阐述了其在水资源管理方面的五项承诺，试图缓解民众对数据中心环境影响的担忧。这些承诺包括：到2030年实现数据中心补水量超过用水量、投资当地水资源基础设施、寻找替代水源、以及全面公开用水数据。谷歌全球基础设施与可持续发展主管本·汤森在接受采访时表示，希望这份“蓝图”能为社区提供参考标准，让其他公司建设数据中心时也能遵循类似原则。

这一举措的背景是美国对AI数据中心建设的抵触情绪日益高涨。谷歌母公司Alphabet近期计划通过股票销售筹集800亿美元以支持AI基础设施建设，而盖洛普最新民调显示，超过70%的美国民众反对在其居住地附近建设数据中心，其中一半反对者提及环境影响，18%特别关注过度用水问题。AI数据中心的冷却系统确实消耗大量水资源，有研究指出其年用水量相当于全球瓶装水消费总量。谷歌此前对自身AI用水量的估算也遭到质疑，研究人员认为其忽略了间接用水。

针对这些争议，谷歌强调其用水管理的进步。汤森称，公司已尽力核算场外水足迹，并通过无水可再生能源投资显著减少了供应链的水消耗。在博客中，谷歌全球基础设施副总裁比卡什·科莱指出，水冷系统相比空冷可降低数据中心约10%的能耗，而美国数据中心的总用水量还不到美国人每年草坪灌溉用水的1%。尽管如此，谷歌仍承诺在未来四年内通过改善灌溉和基础设施等项目实现补水量超过消耗量，并继续每年公布用水数据，寻找如再生废水等替代水源。公司还宣布投入1700万美元支持七个州的水资源管理项目。

汤森承认民众对数据中心“吸干”水资源的担忧是合理的，但也表示实际用水量可能低于大众想象。他强调，现在正是投资确保数据中心用水不成为问题的关键时期，而“说人们对数据中心只有误解是不公平的”。谷歌希望通过这些透明化的行动和实际投资，为整个行业树立一个更可持续的标杆。

history.title: Industrial Software Leaders Build Secure, Autonomous AI Engineers With NVIDIA NemoClaw
history.description: Accelerated computing has revolutionized industrial engineering, compressing simulation times from weeks to hours.  Today’s remaining challenges sit in the end-to-end workflow surrounding the simulations: computer-aided design, meshing, simulation setup and debugging, as well as post-processing and generating summary reports of these processes.  At GTC Taipei at COMPUTEX, NVIDIA and more than a dozen engineering software […]
history.content: Accelerated computing has revolutionized industrial engineering, compressing simulation times from weeks to hours. Today’s remaining challenges sit in the end-to-end workflow surrounding the simulations: computer-aided design, meshing, simulation setup and debugging, as well as post-processing and generating summary reports of these processes. At GTC Taipei at COMPUTEX, NVIDIA and more than a dozen engineering software providers are showcasing how autonomous AI agents automate this entire workflow. These AI engineers are based on NVIDIA NemoClaw, an open blueprint for building specialized, long-running agents with a secure runtime and frontier models. NemoClaw includes a choice of harness — meaning it can be integrated with various orchestration frameworks enterprises use to deploy and coordinate agents, such as OpenClaw and Hermes — as well as a model router and NVIDIA NeMo libraries for customization. Users can easily deploy NemoClaw from NVIDIA DGX Spark personal AI supercomputers, as well as through enterprise data centers and cloud service providers. NVIDIA OpenShell — the open source runtime at its core — governs how each agent accesses files, networks and tools, enforcing policy-based security at every layer. Industrial Engineering Leaders Build AI Agents Across Design, Engineering, Simulation Industrial software leaders are building AI engineers for computer-aided engineering (CAE) and electronic design automation (EDA) use cases across automotive, aerospace, semiconductors and manufacturing. Cadence is building an autonomous register-transfer level (RTL) engineer with NemoClaw that orchestrates Cadence Design Systems ChipStack for design and verification. The workflow was featured yesterday in a GTC Taipei keynote demo and is cutting time for RTL verification — a key step in digital circuit design — from weeks to hours. Dassault Systèmes is actively productizing the 3DEXPERIENCE Agentic Platform to operate long-running and autonomous agents for design, simulation and manufacturing operations, in a secured environment powered by NVIDIA NemoClaw and OpenShell. Siemens is integrating NVIDIA NemoClaw and OpenShell into Fuse EDA AI Agent, a purpose-built autonomous agent that plans and orchestrates domain-scoped multi-tool workflows across semiconductor, 3D integrated circuit and printed circuit board system design. Synopsys is collaborating with NVIDIA to apply agents to end-to-end engineering workflows with NVIDIA NemoClaw. Ansys Icepak, part of the Synopsys portfolio, is being demoed on the COMPUTEX show floor this week, used within a NemoClaw-based autonomous AI engineer to mesh, simulate and optimize GPU electronics cooling designs. Image courtesy of Synopsys. Startups Extend the Reach of Agentic AI In addition, cutting-edge startups are building AI engineers for their workflows — all using NVIDIA NemoClaw. Flexcompute is applying OpenShell to its Tidy3D and PhotonForge agents for multiphysics co-packaged optics design. Flexcompute’s autonomous AI workflow combines optical, electrical and thermal simulation to explore thousands of design variants overnight, producing higher-performing components with lower energy consumption. NVIDIA is using Flexcompute technology for the design and optimization of advanced optical and photonic devices. https://blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/06/flexcompute-video-cut-1.mp4 Video courtesy of Flexcompute. Luminary is building a long-running AI engineer using NemoClaw to dramatically reduce the time and complexity of training AI physics models by autonomously orchestrating data generation, machine learning model selection, and training and re-training loops. https://blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/06/luminary-video-cut.mp4 Video courtesy of Luminary. Neural Concept is deploying an agent for electric motor design. The workflow chains electromagnetic, structural and noise, vibration and harness simulations in a multistep engineering pipeline. https://blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/06/neural-concept-video-cut.mp4 Video courtesy of Neural Concept. nTop, the geometry engine behind JetZero’s blended-wing-body aircraft program, is using NVIDIA NemoClaw to run autonomous design workflows that compress days of geometry iteration into hours. https://blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/06/ntop-video-cut.mp4 Video courtesy of nTop. PhysicsX is partnering with the Microsoft Surface team to build an electronics thermal simulation agent that compresses weeks of manual CAE workflows into automated, AI-driven design cycles. Bringing together the PhysicsX platform, Microsoft Discovery and NVIDIA NemoClaw, the agent automates the full thermal simulation lifecycle for consumer devices such as Microsoft Surface laptops — from mesh sensitivity analysis and simulation data generation, through physics AI model training and optimization-loop execution, to continuous accuracy monitoring across the design exploration process. https://blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/06/physicx-video-cut.mp4 Video courtesy of PhysicsX. P-1 AI is building Archie, an AI mechanical and electrical engineer that already works with data center cooling and critical power systems, and will soon work for automotive, aerospace and national security use cases. In a workflow representative of its work with Daikin Applied Americas, Archie synthesizes requirements, selects components, runs design trade studies and produces engineering artifacts to help industrial manufacturers scale engineering capacity. https://blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/06/p-1-ai-video-cut.mp4 Video courtesy of P-1 AI. SimScale is adopting NVIDIA NemoClaw to build autonomous simulation agents for hundreds of cross-industry engineering use cases, including noise, vibration and harshness analysis, automating workflows that previously required multiple engineers working over several weeks. https://blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/06/simscale-video-cut.mp4 Video courtesy of SimScale. Synera is building an engineering agent for injection molding — a manufacturing process used to efficiently mass-produce identical parts by injecting molten material, usually plastic, into a custom mold — with Autodesk Moldflow, NVIDIA OpenShell with OpenClaw, as well as Nemotron models. https://blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/06/synera-video-cut.mp4 Video courtesy of Synera. Learn more about NVIDIA technologies for CAE and watch NVIDIA founder and CEO Jensen Huang’s GTC Taipei keynote in replay.
history.author: Timothy Costa
history.titleZh: 工业软件领导者利用NVIDIA NemoClaw构建安全、自主的AI工程师
history.descriptionZh:
加速计算已经彻底改变了工业工程领域，将仿真时间从数周压缩至数小时。然而，如今仍存的挑战集中在仿真流程的端到端工作流中：计算机辅助设计、网格划分、仿真设置与调试，以及后处理和生成这些过程的总结报告。在GTC Taipei与COMPUTEX同期举办的活动中，NVIDIA与十余家工程软件提供商共同展示了如何通过自主AI智能体自动化整个工作流。这些AI工程师基于NVIDIA NemoClaw构建——这是一个开放的蓝图，用于构建专用、长时间运行的智能体，配备安全运行时和前沿模型。NemoClaw包含一个可选“套件”（harness），意味着它可以与企业用于部署和协调智能体的多种编排框架（如OpenClaw和Hermes）集成，此外还包含模型路由器和NVIDIA NeMo库以便定制。用户可以从NVIDIA DGX Spark个人AI超级计算机，以及通过企业数据中心和云服务提供商轻松部署NemoClaw。其核心的开源运行时NVIDIA OpenShell，负责管控每个智能体如何访问文件、网络和工具，并在每一层执行基于策略的安全控制。

### 工业工程领导者构建跨设计、工程、仿真的AI智能体

工业软件领导者们正在为汽车、航空航天、半导体和制造等领域的计算机辅助工程（CAE）和电子设计自动化（EDA）用例构建AI工程师。Cadence正利用NemoClaw构建一个自主的寄存器传输级（RTL）工程师，该智能体协调Cadence Design Systems的ChipStack进行设计与验证。该工作流在昨日GTC Taipei主题演讲演示中亮相，将RTL验证（数字电路设计的关键步骤）的时间从数周缩短至数小时。Dassault Systèmes正在积极产品化3DEXPERIENCE智能体平台，以运行长时间运行的自主智能体，用于设计、仿真和制造操作，运行环境由NVIDIA NemoClaw和OpenShell提供安全保障。Siemens正在将NVIDIA NemoClaw和OpenShell集成到Fuse EDA AI Agent中，这是一个专门构建的自主智能体，可在半导体、3D集成电路和印刷电路板系统设计中规划和编排领域限定范围内的多工具工作流。Synopsys正与NVIDIA合作，利用NVIDIA NemoClaw将智能体应用于端到端工程工作流。本周在COMPUTEX展台上，展示了Ansys Icepak（Synopsys产品组合的一部分）被用于一个基于NemoClaw的自主AI工程师中，以进行网格划分、仿真和优化GPU电子散热设计。

### 初创企业拓展智能体AI的应用范围

此外，前沿初创公司也在为其工作流构建AI工程师——全部使用NVIDIA NemoClaw。Flexcompute正在将OpenShell应用于其Tidy3D和PhotonForge智能体，用于多物理场共封装光学设计。Flexcompute的自主AI工作流结合了光学、电学和热学仿真，可在夜间探索数千种设计变体，从而以更低的能耗生产性能更高的组件。NVIDIA正在使用Flexcompute技术进行先进光学和光子器件的设计与优化。Luminary正在使用NemoClaw构建一个长时间运行的AI工程师，通过自主编排数据生成、机器学习模型选择以及训练和重新训练循环，大幅减少训练AI物理模型所需的时间和复杂性。Neural Concept正在部署一个用于电机设计的智能体。该工作流将电磁、结构以及噪声、振动和冲击仿真串联成一个多步骤的工程流水线。nTop（JetZero混合翼身飞机项目的几何引擎）正使用NVIDIA NemoClaw运行自主设计工作流，将数天的几何迭代压缩至数小时。PhysicsX正与微软Surface团队合作，构建一个电子热仿真智能体，将数周的手动CAE工作流压缩为自动化、AI驱动的设计周期。该智能体结合了PhysicsX平台、Microsoft Discovery和NVIDIA NemoClaw，自动完成消费设备（如微软Surface笔记本电脑）的完整热仿真生命周期——从网格敏感性分析和仿真数据生成，到物理AI模型训练和优化循环执行，再到设计探索过程中的持续精度监控。P-1 AI正在构建Archie，一个AI机械和电气工程师，它已用于数据中心冷却和关键电源系统，并将很快应用于汽车、航空航天和国家安全场景。在与Daikin Applied Americas合作的一个典型工作流中，Archie综合需求、选择组件、运行设计权衡研究并生成工程制品，帮助工业制造商扩大工程能力。SimScale正在采用NVIDIA NemoClaw，为数百个跨行业工程用例构建自主仿真智能体，包括噪声、振动和冲击分析，自动化了以往需要多名工程师花费数周才能完成的工作流。Synera正在构建一个注塑成型（一种通过将熔融材料（通常是塑料）注入定制模具来高效批量生产相同零件的制造工艺）的工程智能体，该智能体集成了Autodesk Moldflow、NVIDIA OpenShell与OpenClaw以及Nemotron模型。

了解更多关于NVIDIA CAE技术的信息，并观看NVIDIA创始人兼CEO黄仁勋在GTC Taipei的主题演讲回放。

history.titleZh: 在半导体制造中使用图注意力进行虚拟计量（英特尔代工，亚利桑那州立大学）
history.descriptionZh:
来自亚利桑那州立大学和英特尔代工（Intel Foundry）的研究人员联合发表了题为《基于图注意力机制的半导体制造薄膜沉积过程虚拟计量》的研究论文。摘要指出，人工智能驱动的半导体制造正逐步迈向纳米乃至埃级尺度，在此尺度下，精确的过程控制依赖于准确且及时的计量。然而，物理计量受限于测量延迟、成本以及采样约束，难以满足大规模、高精度生产的需求。为此，研究团队提出了一种基于图注意力网络的虚拟计量方法，用于预测薄膜沉积过程中的关键参数，从而替代或补充传统物理测量。

核心技术原理上，该方法创新性地将半导体制造过程中各工艺参数（如温度、压力、气体流量等）及其相互作用建模为图结构数据，其中节点代表不同工艺变量或设备状态，边则表征变量之间的耦合关系。在此基础上引入图注意力机制，使模型能够动态学习不同节点之间的重要性权重，自动聚焦对薄膜沉积质量影响最大的关键变量及其时序变化。与传统机器学习模型（如随机森林、支持向量机）或普通图神经网络相比，图注意力网络不仅捕捉了工艺参数间的复杂非线性关系，还解决了固定权重难以适应工艺漂移的问题，显著提升了虚拟计量的泛化能力和鲁棒性。

在性能参数与对比数据方面，研究团队在英特尔代工的实际量产薄膜沉积设备上进行了验证。实验结果表明，该图注意力模型在预测膜厚均匀性、沉积速率及颗粒缺陷密度等关键指标上，预测误差比传统回归模型降低约30%-50%，同时将虚拟计量的响应时间从数小时（依赖物理采样与离线检测）缩短至毫秒级，实现了近乎实时的过程监控。与未引入注意力机制的图卷积网络相比，该模型在各项指标上均提升了约15%-20%的准确率，且对异常工艺状态的预警灵敏度更高。

这项技术的核心影响体现在：首先，它大幅降低了对高成本、高延迟物理计量的依赖，使得晶圆厂可以在不增加检测设备投入的情况下，对每一片晶圆的薄膜沉积质量进行全数预测，从而提升良率并降低制造成本。其次，图注意力机制的可解释性使工程师能够直观识别导致工艺偏差的关键变量，辅助工艺工程师进行根因分析与参数调优。应用场景覆盖了从先进逻辑制程（如3纳米、2纳米节点）到3D NAND、DRAM等存储芯片的薄膜沉积步骤，尤其适用于原子层沉积和化学气相沉积等对均匀性和厚度控制要求极为严苛的工艺环节。此外，该框架也可扩展至刻蚀、光刻等其他半导体制造工序，有望成为AI驱动智能制造中虚拟计量领域的通用技术基石。
